sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: Let${ o}$be an associative ring with identity. A system of its ideals${ A}=\{ { A}_{ij} i,j { Z}\}$is called a carpet of ideals\/ if${ A}_{ik}{ A}_{kj} { A}_{ij}$for all$i,j,k { Z}$. If${ o}$is commutative, then the set$_n(A)=\{ x SL_n({ o}) x_{ij} _{ij}\, ({ mod}\, { A}_{ij})\}$is a group, the$($special\/$)$congruenz-subgroup modulo the carpet${ A}$(the ``carpet subgroup"). Under quite general co...
